claim: NFL players did not stand for the national anthem until the Defense Dept. started paying the league to stage patriotic displays in 2009.
reports: Several players last season followed in the footsteps of former San Francisco 49ers quarterback Colin Kaepernick, who in the previous year chose to kneel during the national anthem as a form of protest against police brutality and racial inequality.
It's a tribute to the NFL's ability to drape itself in the flag that nobody even realized that prior to 2009, players being on the field for the national anthem wasn't even standard practice.
label: half-true
